To prevent Ezekiel from being proud because of the many revelations he received, he is reminded that he remains a human being, a weak and mortal creature. As Christ often called himself the Son of man, this was also an honorable distinction. Ezekiel’s posture showed reverence, but standing up would be a more ready and suitable position for work. God will speak to us when we are prepared to do what he commands. Because Ezekiel did not have strength on his own, the Spirit entered him. God is graciously pleased to work in us whatever he requires of us. The Holy Spirit sets us upon our feet by inclining our wills to our duty. Thus, when the Lord calls upon a sinner to awaken and attend to the concerns of their soul, the Spirit of life and grace comes with that call. Ezekiel is sent with a message to the children of Israel. Many may treat his message with contempt, yet they should know from what happens that a prophet has been sent to them. God will be glorified, and his word made honorable, whether it is a fragrance of life leading to life or of death leading to death.

Those who want to accomplish anything worthwhile in the service of God must not fear people. Wicked people are like briars and thorns; but they are close to cursing, and their end will be burning. The prophet must be faithful to the souls of those to whom he was sent. All who speak from God to others must obey his voice. Discoveries of sin and warnings of wrath should be a cause for lamentation. And those familiar with the word of God will clearly see that it is filled with sorrow for unrepentant sinners; and that all the precious promises of the gospel are for the repenting, believing servants of the Lord.
